paragraph: 2. Education must help young people to gain a better understanding of the world and its inhabitants and to foster attitudes that develop in them a spirit of mutual appreciation and respect towards other cultures, races, and ways of life. Education must highlight the relationships between the environment and modes and standards of living. While presenting in an objective manner the differences that exist, particularly in political, economic, and social systems, it must emphasize the values, aspirations, and needs common to the existence and consciousness of different peoples.

paragraph: 32. It is important to reorganize, where appropriate, certain activities both within and outside the school curriculum, in order to complement and strengthen education for international understanding throughout the entire educational system. The range of these activities should be broad and varied enough to provide all students with opportunities to exercise and develop their particular skills and interests. It is desirable that among these activities there should be the organization of clubs or associations, such as UNESCO clubs, United Nations clubs, and international relations clubs whose main purpose is to promote international understanding, as well as the celebration of anniversaries of individuals who have made outstanding contributions to the scientific and cultural development of humanity, and commemorative days marking events of international significance, such as United Nations Day and Human Rights Day. The clubs should organize interschool activities and form national and regional associations. Initiatives taken by pupils and students, as well as their organizations, to undertake new activities should be encouraged. Awards given to the best students could, whenever possible, take the form of travel opportunities allowing them to visit other countries.

It is essential that teachers be prepared as comprehensively as possible from moral, psychological, and professional perspectives to effectively fulfill their role in education for international understanding. Appropriate methods should be studied to guide teacher training courses and education in general according to the objectives of international understanding. Teacher training must, in particular, aim to minimize the influence of their own prejudices and to develop mental and behavioral qualities that will enhance their effectiveness as educators for international understanding. Moreover, training should seek to awaken students’ interest in this aspect of education, convince them of its importance, and inspire in them the belief that they are capable of teaching in this spirit. An imaginative effort is required to develop new approaches for the preparation and continuing education of teachers. Institutions responsible for teacher preparation should be encouraged, with the participation of teachers and students, to design projects and educational programs for international understanding that are adapted to specific circumstances.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 9. In terms of education, the study of the environment contributes: a) to inspiring respect and love for nature; b) to strengthening the links between manual and intellectual work; c) to preparing youth for their future responsibilities through a better understanding of the conditions and possibilities of human life in a rapidly changing world; d) to bringing the school environment closer to the family and social environment in order to establish better collaboration for the benefit of either; e) to harmoniously integrating the child, both the typical child and the child with disabilities, into their immediate surroundings, and the adolescent into the broader natural and economic environment; f) to improving classroom spirit by establishing closer relationships between teacher and students based on cooperation and solidarity; g) to guiding destinies through the revelation of tastes and aptitudes that manifest during various activities; h) to encouraging youth to become more aware of an ideal of international solidarity and an understanding of the serious problems of the current world, thereby contributing to the assumption of responsibility by individuals and communities in relation to these issues.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes environmental education's role in fostering respect for nature, integrating children into surroundings, and guiding destinies through aptitudes, aligning with general academic orientation. Vocational/technical categories are excluded as the focus is on holistic development, not specific skills or TVET. Combined general and vocational is not selected due to lack of explicit vocational components.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes life skills through cooperation, solidarity, and responsibility (21st century competencies). It mentions inclusive education by addressing children with disabilities and harmonious integration. Global citizenship is addressed through international solidarity and awareness of global issues.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph outlines environmental education's role in curriculum integration (Curriculum and pedagogy policies), aligns with sustainable development goals (Sustainable development and environment), addresses inclusion of diverse learners (Inclusion and diversity), and promotes international solidarity (International cooperation and partnerships). Excluded categories like 'Governance' or 'Financing' lack direct mention, and 'Education in emergencies' is irrelevant here.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes education's role in preparing youth (learners/students), integrating children into surroundings (parents/communities), and improving classroom spirit through teacher-student relationships (teachers/educators). 'Policy theme' was excluded as the focus is on educational impacts rather than policy directives.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph outlines specific learning outcomes (a-g) and content areas related to environmental education, supporting 'Content and learning areas'. It mentions integrating education into school and social environments, indicating curriculum implementation. References to guiding destinies and developing aptitudes align with competency-based outcomes.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 10. The study of the environment should: a) pay particular attention to the problems of urban areas to ensure the protection of their inhabitants against the dangers that threaten them, for example: air and water pollution; b) make children in rural areas aware of the possibilities and difficulties of life in the countryside and of the necessity for its inhabitants to actively contribute to the improvement of their living conditions.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes environmental awareness and understanding of urban/rural living, aligning with general academic education. It also includes practical awareness of rural challenges and active participation, indicating vocational elements. 'Combined general and vocational' is supported by the dual focus on knowledge and applied awareness.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'children in rural areas' and focuses on environmental education for both urban and rural children, supporting 'Children (0-17)' and 'Rural and remote learners'. 'Urban areas' are implied but not explicitly labeled as a category here.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes environmental education (sustainable development) and active participation in improving living conditions, aligning with life skills. Citizenship and civic education is excluded as the focus is on environmental awareness, not civic duties. Global citizenship is less relevant here.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: A sound understanding of the environment and of the relationship between humans and their surroundings must form the basis for the development of curricula. These should be designed with due regard to the need to facilitate a smooth transition from traditional attitudes to those demanded by science and technology, while remaining adapted to the specific conditions of the various communities.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum design and adaptation to community needs, supporting 'Design and development'. It also discusses content related to environmental understanding and scientific attitudes, aligning with 'Content and learning areas'. 'Curriculum implementation' and 'evaluation' are not explicitly mentioned. 'Cross-cutting themes' and 'education orientation' are relevant but not directly labeled in the text.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes the relationship between humans and their environment, aligning with 'Sustainable development and climate action'. It also mentions adapting curricula to scientific attitudes and community-specific conditions, which relates to 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. Other categories like 'Citizenship' or 'Digital literacy' are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum development based on environmental understanding and scientific attitudes, aligning with general academic orientation. Vocational/technical (TVET) is excluded as there's no mention of job-specific skills. Combined general and vocational is rejected due to lack of explicit vocational focus.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph explicitly mentions curriculum development and adapting to environmental understanding, directly aligning with 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' and 'Sustainable development and environment'. Other categories like 'Education orientation' and 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' are indirectly related but not as directly supported as the selected categories.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 12. The study and understanding of the environment should be approached through active methods that make the best use of the abilities of both students and teachers, as well as their working conditions within the framework of their class curriculum. They should give rise to interdisciplinary activities to enable comprehensive learning.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes active methods and interdisciplinary activities within the curriculum framework, aligning with 'Design and development' for curriculum structure and 'Content and learning areas' for interdisciplinary learning. 'Curriculum implementation' and 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' are less directly supported. 'Competency-based and learning outcomes' is not explicitly mentioned, and 'Other curriculum-related issue' is avoided due to specificity.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes active methods and interdisciplinary activities, aligning with 'Teaching methods and approaches'. It also mentions curriculum integration and holistic learning, supporting 'Pedagogical foundations and theories'. 'Assessment and feedback' is excluded as there's no mention of evaluation. 'Professional learning communities' and 'Technology-supported pedagogy' are not explicitly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes active, interdisciplinary learning and holistic approaches, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' for active methods. 'Sustainable development and climate action' is included due to the focus on environmental education. 'Inclusive education and special needs' is relevant as it mentions leveraging students' abilities and working conditions. Other categories like 'Global citizenship' or 'Digital literacy' are not explicitly supported.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 13. Collective or individual observation exercises, whether free or guided, encourage intellectual curiosity and inquiry, fostering the desire and will to know and understand.

low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph emphasizes observation exercises, which align with informal learning's emphasis on spontaneous, experiential knowledge acquisition. 'Formal education' and 'Non-formal education' are excluded as the text lacks structured curricula or institutional context. 'Lifelong learning' is not explicitly mentioned, though curiosity-fostering could relate, but the primary focus is on observation methods rather than learning duration or stages.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes observation exercises as pedagogical practices that foster curiosity and inquiry, aligning with 'Pedagogical foundations and theories' and 'Teaching methods and approaches'. 'Assessment and feedback' is excluded as the focus is on inquiry rather than evaluation. 'Learning modality' is rejected as the text does not discuss delivery formats.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 14. Drawing and music, painting and games, the classroom’s “living corner” are all means likely to stimulate interest in a better understanding of the environment.

low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes diverse activities (drawing, music, games) as means to stimulate environmental understanding, aligning with 'Pedagogical foundations and theories' and 'Teaching methods and approaches'. 'Assessment and feedback' is excluded as there's no mention of evaluation. 'Curriculum' and 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' are not directly addressed in the paragraph.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes engaging activities (drawing, music, games) that foster environmental understanding, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' for holistic development. 'Sustainable development and climate action' is directly referenced through the environmental focus. Other categories like citizenship or digital literacy lack explicit mention in the text.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 15. The study of the neighborhood or village, guided visits to museums, public buildings, farms, factories, etc., will be facilitated as much as possible.

low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph describes experiential activities like guided visits to museums and farms, which align with informal learning's hands-on, context-based approach. Non-formal education is also applicable as these activities are structured but not part of formal schooling. Formal education is excluded as the activities are not part of a formal curriculum. Lifelong learning is less directly supported as the text focuses on specific, localized learning experiences rather than continuous learning across life stages.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes experiential learning through guided visits and hands-on activities, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is included due to the holistic, facilitation-focused approach mentioned in the summary. Categories like 'Citizenship and civic education' and 'Sustainable development' are excluded as the text lacks direct references to civic engagement or environmental themes.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 16. Sufficient time should be allocated to educational walks and excursions, and provisions should even be made for students of different ages to stay for a certain period in another natural and social environment: snow, sea, and mountain classes, school camps bringing together children and adolescents from different regions or countries where direct and systematic study of the environment can take place.

low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph describes educational walks, excursions, and school camps as structured yet experiential learning opportunities outside traditional classrooms, aligning with 'Non-formal education' and 'Informal learning'. 'Formal education' is excluded as it refers to structured, institutionalized learning, while 'Lifelong learning' is not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes experiential learning in natural/social environments, aligning with life skills and 21st century competencies. 'Sustainable development and climate action' is supported by references to environmental study. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is included due to the unique focus on educational walks/excursions not fitting neatly into other categories.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes allocating sufficient time for educational walks/excursions and structured stays in different environments, aligning with 'Organization and instructional time'. It also mentions systematic study in specific settings, indicating curriculum implementation strategies. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is excluded as there's no mention of assessment or reform. 'Design and development' is less fitting since the focus is on time allocation rather than curriculum design.
low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph references educational walks and excursions, which align with primary education's hands-on learning approach. 'Multiple levels' is included due to mention of varying age groups. Secondary/tertiary categories are excluded as the text lacks specific references to higher education stages.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph mentions 'children and adolescents' explicitly, supporting both categories. 'Students' and 'school camps' imply educational settings for younger learners. 'Youth and adolescents' is included as 'adolescents' are part of this category. Other categories like 'All learners' are too broad and not specifically supported by the text.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 17. Inter-school correspondence, the lending of books, the exchange of documents, school newspapers, and local products with classes from other regions or countries should be encouraged as a contribution to the knowledge of a different environment and the development of international understanding.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes international understanding and inter-school collaboration, aligning with 'Global citizenship and peace education'. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' fits due to the focus on developing skills through cross-regional exchanges. 'Other cross-cutting theme' was excluded as specific categories better capture the intent.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes inter-school collaboration and international understanding through shared resources like books and newspapers, aligning with general academic exchange rather than vocational training. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded as there's no mention of technical skills. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is rejected due to lack of focus on job-specific training.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph mentions exchange of documents, books, and local products, aligning with 'Teaching and learning materials'. 'ICT and digital infrastructure' is implied through inter-school correspondence and digital document exchange. Other categories like school buildings or WASH facilities are not mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes inter-school collaboration and international understanding, aligning with 'International cooperation and partnerships'. 'Inclusion and diversity' is supported by the focus on different environments and exchange of perspectives. Other categories like 'Education in emergencies' or 'Sustainable development' are not directly mentioned.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 18. As a general rule, extracurricular or out-of-school activities that can facilitate the study and understanding of the environment should be utilized for this purpose by the teachers who oversee them.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes using extracurricular activities to facilitate environmental understanding, aligning with general academic integration. Vocational/technical categories are excluded as the focus is on broader environmental education rather than specific skills. Combined categories are not selected due to lack of explicit vocational reference.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes environmental education through extracurricular activities, aligning with 'Sustainable development and climate action'. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' fits as it refers to skills developed through these activities. Other categories like citizenship or digital literacy are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph emphasizes teachers' oversight role in environmental activities, which aligns with 'Teacher other' as it captures unspecified roles. Other categories like 'Professional development' or 'Curriculum' are not directly mentioned. 'Teacher supply and shortages' and 'Working conditions' are irrelevant here. The summary's focus on teachers' oversight supports 'Teacher other' over more specific categories.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph discusses utilizing extracurricular activities to facilitate environmental understanding, aligning with 'Curriculum implementation' through teacher oversight. 'Content and learning areas' is supported by the focus on environmental study. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is excluded as there's no mention of assessment or reform. 'Design and development' is less fitting as it doesn't address implementation or content specifics.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes using extracurricular activities to facilitate environmental learning, aligning with pedagogical foundations and teaching methods. 'Pedagogical foundations and theories' reflects the normative statement on integrating environmental education. 'Teaching methods and approaches' matches the use of activities to enhance understanding. 'Assessment and feedback' is excluded as no evaluation mechanisms are mentioned.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 19. Audio-visual aids, including radio and television broadcasts, are particularly useful for supplementing the study of the immediate or distant environment. It would be desirable for sufficient funding to be allocated to develop these resources and make them more widely accessible to schools.

low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph mentions 'schools' and 'sufficient funding to develop these resources', aligning with 'Formal education'. 'Audio-visual aids' and 'accessibility' also fit 'Non-formal education' as they refer to supplementary resources beyond traditional classroom settings. 'Informal learning' and 'Lifelong learning' are excluded as the text focuses on structured educational contexts.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph mentions audio-visual aids (radio/TV) which fall under ICT infrastructure. It also refers to developing resources for schools, aligning with teaching materials. Other categories like school buildings or WASH are not mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes funding allocation for audio-visual resources (Financing), their role in enhancing learning outcomes (Quality), and the use of technology (Innovation). Governance and planning is excluded as the focus is on resource allocation, not systemic planning. Access and equity is not explicitly mentioned, and other categories like infrastructure are not directly addressed.

TITLE: L'étude du milieu à l'école
paragraph: 20. The production of textbooks and reading books devoted to the environment, likely to enhance interest in its understanding, should be encouraged.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph focuses on encouraging environmental education materials (content/learning areas) and implies implementation through textbook production. 'Curriculum implementation' aligns with promoting these materials. 'Other curriculum-related issue' was excluded as the focus is specific to content and implementation, not broader systemic issues.
low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph explicitly mentions environmental education materials (textbooks and reading books) aimed at enhancing understanding of the environment. Other categories like STEM or social sciences are not directly referenced. 'Other subject area' is avoided due to the clear thematic focus on environmental education.
